title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Council urges police to release bodycam footage after fatal shooting of carjacking suspect in Roxbury.
Councilors Culpepper and Worrell lead resolution for timely access to police footage, aiming for transparency and trust.
U.S. Rep. Ayanna Pressley supports calls for releasing footage, emphasizing need for accountability and full disclosure.

title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: New England's only dual-launch straddle coaster, Quantum Accelerator, opens at Six Flags New England on April 11.
The coaster features steampunk and time travel themes, with two launches reaching speeds of 30 and 45 mph.
Quantum Accelerator is Six Flags New England's twelfth rollercoaster and the first new addition since 2017.

title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Public Schools to require AI proficiency in all high school graduates, starting fall 2026.
The AI program is funded by a $1 million grant from Kayak co-founder Paul English.
Program includes teacher training by UMass Boston and ethical AI curriculum development.

title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a court filing to correct a footnote suggesting his brother fathered Sandra Birchmore's unborn child.
The corrected filing clarified that the biological father is unnamed, while Farwell's brother, also a defendant, was involved with Birchmore.
Farwell, charged with killing Birchmore, is seeking release on $50,000 bail pending his trial scheduled for October.

title: A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lily Penzone, 21, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her best friend Abby's late father.
Abby’s father, a former Marathoner, passed away in a trauma accident; Penzone is supporting Brigham and Women's trauma research.
The marathon symbolizes the support Abby's father showed, with funds aiding causes now significant to Penzone and her friends.

title: Boston College senior running Boston Marathon to support first responders
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston College senior Merideth Greene is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Last Call Foundation.
Greene aims to honor first responders, particularly inspired by the Boston Fire Department's role during the 2013 Marathon.
The Last Call Foundation supports firefighter health through cancer screenings, safer gear, and preventive care advocacy.

title: Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Peach Farm, a Chinatown institution, is closing temporarily as owners retire after 31 years and new ownership takes over.
The new manager is Washan Ching, and the Boston Licensing Board approved the license transfer this month.
Peach Farm plans to maintain its name and concept under new ownership, with reopening details yet to be confirmed.

title: Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: U.S. Attorney Leah Foley launches a task force to tackle SNAP, MassHealth, childcare subsidies, and election-related fraud in Massachusetts.
The Benefit and Voter Fraud Team introduces a public hotline, appoints federal attorneys for coordination and prosecution of fraudulent activities.
An ACLU spokesperson criticized the inclusion of voter fraud, labeling it a myth and expressing concern over election integrity rhetoric.

title: United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines announced the Relax Row, a couch-style economy seating, available starting in 2027.
The Relax Row transforms three economy seats into a lie-flat space with adjustable leg rests for added comfort.
United plans to offer the Relax Row on over 200 aircraft, including Boeing 787 and 777, by 2030.

title: Ad Club’s Women’s Leadership Forum stays alive amid DEI pushback
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Women's Leadership Forum by the Ad Club was held at Gillette Stadium, drawing over 1,000 participants.
Despite challenges, the event focused on women's workplace issues amid changing corporate DEI commitments.
Speakers, including Nobel laureate Nadia Murad, emphasized resilience and defining personal success.
